race_name: L'Etape Chile by Tour de France

top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic L'Etape Chile by Tour de France's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before November.
L'Etape Chile by Tour de France's coastal rollers through lush Valdivian rainforest forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 68 miles.
58ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
When someone attacks on coastal rollers through lush Valdivian rainforest, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
68 miles on coastal rollers through lush Valdivian rainforest burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
At L'Etape Chile by Tour de France, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of L'Etape Chile by Tour de France. Train it until it feels boring.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At L'Etape Chile by Tour de France, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on coastal rollers through lush Valdivian rainforest. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 3,937ft of climbing on coastal rollers through lush Valdivian rainforest does to your power file on race day.
Varied gradients on coastal rollers through lush Valdivian rainforest demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for L'Etape Chile by Tour de France’s 68 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at L'Etape Chile by Tour de France requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on coastal rollers through lush valdivian rainforest to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, Gravel Specific, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 68 miles of coastal rollers through lush valdivian rainforest in Valdivia, Los Rios, Chile.
